metric_definitions.resident_ward_contest_weight_index: mean declared ward age_total / ward age_total. Interprets each ward result as one ward contest; higher = fewer residents per ward result.
metric_definitions.resident_councillor_weight_index_nationa: (ward councillor_count / ward age_total) / (declared-ward councillors / declared-ward residents). Higher = more councillors per resident nationally.
metric_definitions.resident_councillor_weight_index_authori: (ward councillor_count / ward age_total) / (all wards in same authority councillors / all residents in same authority). Isolates malapportionment within each council.
metric_definitions.electorate_ward_contest_weight_index: mean current_total_electorate among wards with data / ward current_total_electorate.

main_findings.interpretation: Reform-won wards have higher raw/national resident voting weight, but the effect disappears when normalised within each authority.
